scene.description: Case Z-999. The last memory the Archive ever recorded — the bookend to the first. A Keeper at this desk, at the end of everything, deciding. You cannot tell if you are remembering this or about to live it.

fragments.label: The Same Desk
fragments.text: PRIME-001 opened on a Keeper arriving at this desk for the first time. This one closes on a Keeper at the same desk for the last time. The wear on the third drawer is identical. It is the same drawer.

fragments.label: The Keeper Deciding
fragments.text: The Keeper in this memory is at the exact moment you're at now — facing the closure protocol. You watch them decide. You can't hear which one they pick. The sound is reserved for you.

fragments.label: Memory or Prediction
fragments.symbol: You check the timestamp. It reads both 'first record' and 'last record' at once. This memory is the loop seen end-on: the beginning and the ending are the same event, and you are inside it.

fragments.label: Maya at the End
fragments.text: Maya is in this last memory the way she was in the first — present, steady, a little sad. Whatever you choose, she was there for the beginning and she is here for the end. That much never changed across any version.

interpretations.label: It already happened — you're remembering the end
It hasn't happened — you're about to cause it
It is the loop itself — first and last, always now
